import json
import re

# Get the business data from previous query
business_data = var_functions.query_db:2

# Let's analyze the description field to extract state information
states = []
for business in business_data:
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    # Look for state patterns like "CA," "MO," etc.
    # Pattern: comma followed by space, then 2 uppercase letters, then comma or space
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})[\s,]', desc)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        states.append(state)

state_counts = {}
for state in states:
    state_counts[state] = state_counts.get(state, 0) + 1

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'sample_states': states[:10],
    'state_counts': state_counts,
    'total_businesses_sampled': len(business_data)
}))

import json
import re

# Get all business data
business_data = var_functions.query_db:12

# Extract state information from descriptions
business_states = []
for business in business_data:
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    # Look for state pattern: comma, space, 2 uppercase letters, then comma or space
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})[\s,]', desc)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        business_states.append({
            'business_id': business['business_id'],
            'state': state,
            'review_count': int(business['review_count'])
        })

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_businesses': len(business_data),
    'businesses_with_state': len(business_states),
    'sample_states': [b['state'] for b in business_states[:10]]
}))

import re

print('__RESULT__:')

}))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

import json
import re

# Get the business data
business_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:44']

# Extract state information and business IDs
business_info = []
for business in business_data:
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    # Extract state (2-letter code after comma and space)
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})[\s,]', desc)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

import json
import re

# Get the business data
business_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:44']

# Extract state information and business IDs
business_info = []
for business in business_data:
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    # Extract state (2-letter code after comma and space)
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})[\s,]', desc)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        business_id = business['business_id']
        review_count = int(business['review_count'])
        
        # Extract the numeric part from business_id (e.g., 'businessid_49' -> '49')
        business_num = business_id.split('_')[1]
        
        business_info.append({
            'business_id': business_id,

            'state': state,
            'review_count': review_count
        })

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'business_count': len(business_info),
    'business_info': business_info
}))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
import os

# Get the file path for the review data
review_file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:46']

# Read the full data from the file
with open(review_file_path, 'r') as f:
    review_data = json.load(f)

# Process the data to aggregate reviews by state
from collections import defaultdict

# Create mapping from business_num to state
business_info = locals()['var_functions.execute_python:48']['business_info']
business_to_state = {info['business_num']: info['state'] for info in business_info}

# Aggregate reviews by state
state_reviews = defaultdict(list)
for review in review_data:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    business_num = business_ref.split('_')[1]
    rating = int(review['rating'])
    
    if business_num in business_to_state:
        state = business_to_state[business_num]
        state_reviews[state].append(rating)

# Calculate statistics by state
state_stats = {}
for state, ratings in state_reviews.items():
    state_stats[state] = {
        'total_reviews': len(ratings),
        'avg_rating': sum(ratings) / len(ratings)
    }

# Find state with highest number of reviews
if state_stats:
    top_state = max(state_stats.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]['total_reviews'])
    
    print('__RESULT__:')
    print(json.dumps({
        'top_state': top_state[0],
        'review_count': top_state[1]['total_reviews'],
        'avg_rating': round(top_state[1]['avg_rating'], 2),
        'all_state_stats': state_stats
    }))
else:
    print('__RESULT__:')
    print(json.dumps({'error': 'No matching business found'}))

import json
import os

# Get the file path for the review data
review_file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:46']

# Read the full data from the file
with open(review_file_path, 'r') as f:
    review_data = json.load(f)

# Process the data to aggregate reviews by state
from collections import defaultdict

# Create mapping from business_num to state
business_info = locals()['var_functions.execute_python:48']['business_info']
business_to_state = {info['business_num']: info['state'] for info in business_info}

# Aggregate reviews by state
state_reviews = defaultdict(list)
for review in review_data:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    business_num = business_ref.split('_')[1]
    rating = int(review['rating'])
    
    if business_num in business_to_state:
        state = business_to_state[business_num]
        state_reviews[state].append(rating)

# Calculate statistics by state
state_stats = {}
for state, ratings in state_reviews.items():
    state_stats[state] = {
        'total_reviews': len(ratings),
        'avg_rating': sum(ratings) / len(ratings)
    }

# Find state with highest number of reviews
if state_stats:
    top_state = max(state_stats.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]['total_reviews'])
    
    print('__RESULT__:')
    print(json.dumps({
        'top_state': top_state[0],
        'review_count': top_state[1]['total_reviews'],
        'avg_rating': round(top_state[1]['avg_rating'], 2),
        'all_state_stats': state_stats
    }))
else:
    print('__RESULT__:')
    print(json.dumps({'error': 'No matching business found'}))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!
